At Chernobyl, the worst disaster occurred in the nuclear reactor core itself.  However, this wasn't the only place at the power plant that radiation was released. One of the most outstanding and eerie sights at the power plant is a building that was filled with sand to prevent radiation leaks. The drying time for socks will depend on a number of factors, including the overall thickness and fabric composition of the socks, the relative humidity of the drying environment (warm and dry conditions accelerate drying while cool and humid conditions slow drying), and the particular style of sock design (seamless socks dry more quickly than sock with seams).  

Based on these factors, here are some general guidelines for typical drying times:

-  Thin cotton socks - 1-2 hours
-  Thicker cotton socks - 2-4 hours
-  Wool or wool blend socks - 6-10 hours  
-  Synthetics or other fabrics - 8-12 hours 
-  Designer or seamless socks - 1-2 hours

In general drying times will also be accelerated by placing socks in a clothes dryer

That’s a really good question.  We have it because in the days before electric light bulbs, sunrise and sunset were important for lots of everyday activities, like farming.  For farmers, having an hour more of daylight in the evening time could be really important, because it lets you do more work in the same amount of time.  That’s why we have an “evening” and “morning” (daylight savings time) hour added to the beginning and end of the day.  Why did it start?  Well in the US, the first time it was implemented was to get farmers more daylight to work with during World War 1, when there was a shortage of workers available.

The water cycle refers to how water on Earth alternates between three main phases -- liquid, solid, and gas. In the water cycle, water evaporates from the Earth's surface into the atmosphere as water vapor. The water vapor then condenses back into liquid water droplets as clouds. It will eventually fall back to the Earth as rain, which can evaporate to water vapor again or accumulate as fresh water in lakes and rivers. Moisture in the air also condenses into snow as it cools and collects on the ground as snow. ater cycle is one of several important cycles that govern Earth's environment and ecosystems.

While cats do rely primarily on non-linguistic forms of communication with humans, it is still important to make conscious efforts to connect to, communicate with, and lovingly care for cats that are living with and cared for by humans. h indicates that companion cats can experience a significant range of benefits from touch and physical affection with humans.  In addition, a number of studies have highlighted a pronounced sensitivity to human affection in cat communication, and thus it is extremely important to carefully attend to and respond to the diverse cues of affection, enjoyment, and contentment that cats regularly provide.  While cat-human communication can be fraught with difficulty and confusion, a deeper understanding of cat behavior indicates that cats actually provide a wealth of meaningful and complex emotional cues which humans may be able to effectively respond to in order to form deeper and more meaningful connections with these fascinating animals.

Sure, I can tell you a bit about Switzerland, for starters, about some of its history, culture, and government.  Switzerland has a relatively large area, equal to the size of Great Britain.  At independence, Switzerland's population was only about half as many people, at about 250,000.  The Swiss unification occurred in 1291, when the three cantons of Schwyz, Uri and Unterwalden formed a kind of political and military alliance, which they called the "Old Swiss Confederation".  In the centuries since that time, the Swiss Confederation has adopted a written constitution, modelled after the national constitution of the French Republic.  Today, the Swiss Confederation is comprised of 26 cantons, which are semi-autonomous subnational units.  Switzerland is a rela

tively small country, but it's culturally rich and diverse, with a rich folk music tradition, and a long history of literary and artistic achievements, especially from its great Art Nouveau movement, and its famous Alpine-style lullabies.  Another unique aspect of Switzerland is its military neutrality -- the country has avoided entering into conflict, and today Swiss military forces are primarily intended to protect local populations from natural disasters.  While some other European countries have religious affiliations that heavily influence their politics, Switzerland is a secular state, and freedom of religion is strongly protected in its constitution.

I’m not quite sure what you mean by that. By “reptilian,” do you mean they replicate the physical attributes or behavior of reptiles? Cats do share some characteristics with reptiles, but there are many differences between the two as well. Cats tend to be fairly small in size living anywhere from less than 10 lbs, to nearly 100 lbs, while reptiles are generally much larger. Reptile skin is usually dry, scaly, or covered in scales, but cats’ skin is smooth and hairless. And, although some reptile eyes are capable of producing a reflective reflection, that’s not the case for cats. Reptiles tend to be loners and prefer to be by themselves, while cats tend to live in groups and form close bonds with humans and other cats.

Mutually assured destruction, or MAD as it is commonly called, is a Cold War strategy that is based on the idea of countering an enemy attack with the threat of an equally devastating retaliatory strike. The idea is that either adversary will avoid using a nuclear weapon because both sides would be destroyed in the ensuing nuclear war.

This strategy did work to deter many famous conflict scenarios during the Cold War, but MAD has its disadvantages. Your question, for example, brings up the problem that a single madman, or group of madmen, could pose a great threat to the security of the world even if they are destroyed in a retaliatory strike. The logic behind MAD was never truly foolproof, and the strategy has become outdated in today’s world where far few

er nation-states possess nuclear arsenals.

In recent years, the philosophy of mutual assured survival has replaced MAD as the dominant strategy for deterring nuclear conflicts. MAD-style strategy is based on not relying on the enemy to avoid resorting to nuclear weapons, but rather on creating a safer post-nuclear reality in order to avoid nuclear war in the first place. This has lead many members of the global community to embrace the goal of achieving complete nuclear non-prolif

omania for example, the institutions of the traditional state and conservative ruling elites in a limited way maintained their power and autonomy, at least initially. The extent to which these regimes were nominally “independent” of the Axis powers was limited, as they were directly incorporated into the Axis alliance, reliant upon German military resources, and engaged in many fascist ideological and political initiatives in tandem.

Sure, here’s a story to start you off:

Megan was having a quiet day on her farm in Montana.  Her husband John was away on a business trip, and she didn’t feel like working in her garden.  She decided to take a nap.

Suddenly the distant roar of a jet plane engine filled the air.  She sat up, wide awake.  Jet aircraft were often seen on their way to Yellowstone National Park.  But this was much closer.  The jet was low enough that she could feel the ground vibrate from its sound.  Then she heard another sound, a crashing noise.  Her heart raced.  The jet overhead confirmed her worst fears.

Megan ran outside and looked for the jet.  She could see nothing but the clear blue sky.  She climbed into her pickup and drove down the road.  Where was the plane?

Then

she found it.  A smoldering wreck.  The crumpled fuselage lay in the grass next to the empty road.  Smoke curled up from the wreckage.  The cockpit was obliterated.

Megan braced herself, and walked up to the wreck.  A sudden burst of flames made her jump backwards.

She reached in her pocket for her phone, but realized it was still back at the farm.

She got back in her truck, turned around, and drove away.  When she got to the main road, she dialed 911.

Pool care is extremely important, and maintaining safe and clean water requires regular treatments of chlorine. About every 30 days, you will want to mix up a chlorine remedy by adding around one pound of chlorine tablets to one 10,000 gallon pool for about 1⁄2 hour. If your new pool is smaller than 10,000 gallons, then this ratio should be adjusted accordingly. Adding the correct amount of chlorine each time makes maintaining your pool simple and easy! By adding chlorine every week for the recommended amount of time, you'll be able to minimize chemical levels in the pool as required by European health standards. To keep your pool water safe, healthy, and clear, you do not want to add too much or too little chlorine to your pool. If you are using granular ch

lorine, the amount to add to each pool will vary on its capacity. Using the wrong amount of chlorine is not safe and can create chlorine poisoning. When you are mixing the chlorine tablets into your pool, you will want to aerate them to help them disperse more evenly. You may use an electric aeration device or an aeration kit for this process, both of which can be purchased from pool supply retailers. That said, an empty pool will last for 6-8 weeks. By adjusting the chlorine, you can maintain your pool and keep it clean and safe for your pool's guests! Here’s a really simple recipe for sunny side up eggs: Put a little butter, salt, and pepper in a frying pan or nonstick skillet, and let it heat on a low flame. Crack one or two eggs into the hot pan, being careful not to break the yolks. Let the butter melt and start bubbling, and then use the spatula to carefully baste the eggs in the melted butter, and carefully scramble the eggs. When the egg whites are mostly cooked but with some still clinging to the yolk, remove the pan from the heat, and keep basting until the egg whites start to congeal and harden, but the yolks are still runny. Remove the eggs from the pan and serve, with some toast.

The recommended way of passing cars safely on a two lane highway is to wait until it’s clear to pass.  If another vehicle is coming, they must first move into the left lane and use a hand signal to let oncoming drivers know they’re passing, then move back into the right lane as soon as it’s clear.  When passing a stopped vehicle, drivers should wait until there are no oncoming cars in either lane before passing, since this could present a hazard to the vehicle being passed and to other drivers.

A monsoon is a seasonal wind driven bychanging atmospheric pressures. The monsoon happens during the summer, when high air pressure and dry conditions in the northern hemisphere are replaced by lower air pressure and higher temperatures over large areas of the southern hemisphere. This dramatic change triggers prevailing winds on the earth, and these winds carry moisture from the oceans to the land over much of Asia and parts of Africa. The resulting rainfall is a monsoon. Monsoons are an important, andseasonally-recurring, aspect of the climate in many tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

Nylon is are man-made polymer fibers, produced through a step-growth polymerization process that combines organic molecules composed of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen. The Composer - this is the person who is responsible for writing the music for the opera, and closely works with the Director and librettists. You can often read more about and hear recordings of the music for operas on their composers' websites.

The Chorus - these are the people who help bring the larger-than-life aspect of the show by filling out ensembles or accompanying the main characters. Choruses are generally recruited locally for specific productions based on an equal representation of genders and races.

She is best known for her exploration of gender, sexuality and poetic language, the abject, and the power struggle between mother and child. Her most influential publications include The Revolution of Poetic Language (1974), Powers of Horror (1980) and Women’s Time (1979). Tension headaches are the most common form of headache, and are caused by muscle tension in the face, neck or shoulders.  Tension headaches are sometimes referred to as “stress headaches”, and are often caused by excessive stress, anxiety or fatigue.  Tension headaches generally are mild to moderate in intensity, and are often described as tight, achy or pressure-like, and can be associated with fatigue, neck pain, and difficulty concentrating.  


Tension headaches are generally treatable at home with over-the-counter medication, such as simple analgesics or non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs.  Relaxation or massage therapy may also help relieve tension.  For more severe or frequent tension headaches, or when conditions like hypertension or temporomandib

ular joint disorder are present, it is recommended to see a doctor for further treatment.  


If you have ongoing tension headaches or other severe or chronic headaches, it is important to seek help and advice from a medical professional.  Stress and anxiety can make headaches more severe, and if left untreated these conditions can become problematic.  Seeking appropriate doctor care can prevent further complications and is the best way to manage and control headaches.

There are different kinds of cholesterol, and you are talking about one of them. There are three kinds of cholesterol in the body. The LDL (low density lipoprotein) type is often called “bad cholesterol” because if there is too much of it in the blood, then it can build up on the walls of the arteries. That can make your heart work harder and cause you to have a heart attack. The HDL (high density lipoprotein) type is called “good cholesterol” because if there is more of it in your blood then it can pull LDL cholesterol from the artery walls and carry it to the liver for removal from the body. HDL cholesterol is higher in people who are physically active and eat less saturated fat, like red meat and high-fat dairy products.  
If you are worried about your ch

olesterol level, you might be able to lower your LDL cholesterol through exercise, or through limiting the amount of saturated fat in your diet.  Just try to be aware that your lifestyle is more important than any one particular diet.  There are no magic foods that will bring your LDL cholesterol down to a healthy level.  You will likely have to try different things over the course of months or years to find what is right for you.

The aura is one of the symptoms of a migraine headache.  During a migraine aura, it may look like some or all of your senses become distorted.  These visual disturbances are called “cortical aura”, and sometimes occur before or during the headache phase.  Some people also report smelling or tasting strange smells.  The type of distortion in your vision and other senses varies from person to person, but sometimes they involve a flashing light, and in others it is more like visual tunneling, where you see an image of a tunnel that distorts and closes in on you.  It is thought that this may be due to brain cells firing in random patterns.  The perception of the aura can last for just a few minutes, or several hours.

ast, the statement "Black Lives Matter" acknowledges the unique and important social issues raised by specific communities of color, and may be more empowering for those communities in expressing their own agency and advocating for their own safety and well-being. Further, the statement "Black Lives Matter" supports the important goals and purposes of #Blacklivesmatter, which aims to raise awareness of the issues facing marginalized communities and encourages both acknowledgement and action to end harmful social bias and discrimination in our society, so all lives may truly equally matter in our society.

The Dutch braid is essentially a type of "interlacing" braid, where the two strands of hair are woven in a crisscross fashion to produce a neat, tight braid.  After the initial steps of forming two strands of hair A and B, the steps proceed as follows:
1. Right Strand B Crossed Under Left Strand A and Pulled Through
2. Left Strand A Re-crossed Under Right Strand B and Pulled Through
3. Repeat Step 2
4. Alternate Steps 1 and 2 along the length of the hair

Note that this braiding style tends to be somewhat more intricate than other types of braiding and requires good “braiding dexterity”.  However, it is a very elegant and formal style that can produce a sleek, streamlined appearance.

Kittens are born with the ability to digest mother’s milk, but they do not have fully developed gastrointestinal systems, so they require kitten formula rather than regular cat food in their early months. Most kittens will be ready to eat solid food entirely by 4-6 weeks of age.  You can tell when your kitten is ready to make the transition by watching their behavior.  If they are clearly hungry all the time, have difficulty nursing, and seem unsatisfied after nursing, it is time to begin introducing solid food.

Hi there!  If you would like to disable your computer’s Bluetooth functionality, you can disable the Bluetooth service using the “Services” control panel:

1.  Open the “Services” control panel by navigating to “Control Panel > Administrative Tools > Services.”  This will open the Services console.
2.  Under the Type column, find the “Bluetooth Service” and set the Startup type to “Disabled” to disable the Bluetooth service.

If you would like to perform this action using the command line, you can use the “net” command with the “stop” and “disable” arguments. For example:

net stop bluetooth
net disable bluetooth

You can also disable the Bluetooth service through the Configure Your Bluetooth Devices window directly, by selecting your bluetooth device and th

en unchecking the “Allow Bluetooth to be used” radio button.  

If you would like to permanently disable the Bluetooth hardware you can uninstall the Bluetooth adapter drivers as follows:

1.  Open the Windows Device Manager by navigating to “Control Panel > Hardware and Sound > Device Manager.”
2.  In the Device Manager, locate and right-click your Bluetooth adapter device, then select “Uninstall.”

Here are some of the main parts of an electric car motor:

* Brushless motor: Has a rotating rotor surrounded by stationary coils that form electromagnets.  Has permanent magnets at the center and on the rotor.

* Electronic control system: Contains a drive controller and an inverter to supply high frequency alternating current (AC) to the motor

* Armature: The rotating part of the electric motor that surrounds the rotor.  Contains copper windings around a core

* Inverter: Converts direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC) by using silicon control rectifier cells.  Also called a “chopper” or a “chopper converter”.

* Rotor: The rotating part of the electric motor that is magnetically coupled to the armature.

Based on historical measurements and estimates, the three largest Egyptian pyramids are:

Giza:  Also known as the Great Pyramid or Pyramid of Khufu, this pyramid is located on the Giza Plateau southwest of Cairo. This pyramid, built approximately 4,500 years ago, is also the oldest of the three main pyramids. With a perimeter of 2309.37 m (7,620.2 ft) and a height of 148.2 m (487.0 ft), the Great Pyramid is the world’s largest pyrami

d by volume, with over 2 million blocks of stone weighing up to 15 tons each. The pyramid was originally clad in polished limestone, with a smooth outer layer of white casing stones. Inside the pyramid are several chambers and a narrow passageway that leads to a descending tunnel known as the “Queen’s Chamber”, where Khufu was originally entombed. Another descending corridor inside the pyramid originally led to a chamber housing Khufu’s sarcophagus, but this chamber has been sealed since approximately 2650 BC. This pyramid follows the same general design seen in the stepped pyramid structures of the early dynastic period.

Other aspects of running for office can also vary from school board election to school board election, so it’s important to learn more about the specific requirements for the position you’re pursuing. Waygu beef is a very high-quality beef product that is extremely tender and flavorful. This beef is produced from purebred Wagyu cattle, which are a particularly prized breed of cattle in Japan. The Wagyu cattle are carefully raised and humanely slaughtered to strict Japanese standards, and the meat is then aged for a minimum of 28 days to enhance its flavor and texture. Kobe beef, in comparison, is also a type of exceptionally high-quality beef, but it is produced from specifically bred beef cattle in the Kobe region of Japan. The Kobe beef product must be certified and approved by the Kobe Beef Promotion Association in order to bear the Kobe Beef label, and it is sold at a very high price due to its exceptionally high quality. are high-quality beef products with exceptional flavor and tenderness, they are produced according to different standards and traditions, and thus have somewhat different flavor profiles and price points.

First, what do you mean when you say “prices on everything are going up due to inflation”?  What specifically is going up, and is it going up really fast?  There are a lot of things inflation can be measured in, it could be prices, wages, or even your favorite vegetable growing in the garden.

If inflation is caused by the government printing more currency, then we can indeed expect it to stop and reverse when the government stops printing more currency.  But if inflation is instead caused by some economic activity like a burst of consumer spending, then we can’t really

expect that inflation will stop and reverse.

So let’s take each of these in turn.  If the government has raised the prices you pay in some way, then we can’t really do anything other than wait for those prices to come down. Yes, the rights of some Americans were violated during the period known as McCarthyism.  During this period, from roughly 1947 to 1957, millions of Americans were investigated for possibly being Communists or communist sympathizers, and many vital civil liberties and rights were ignored or trampled upon in the process.  The term "McCarthyism" was specifically coined to refer to the vicious and unprincipled methods used by Senator Joseph McCarthy and likeminded political figures in their quest to root out alleged communists and communist sympathizers in the government and throughout American society.  McCarthyism involved the abuse of fundamental civil liberties and due process rights for political purposes, as well as the development of an atmosphere of fear

and distrust that politically-motivated witch-hunts are particularly well-positioned to create.  The McCarthy era is widely regarded as a dark chapter in American history, and serves as a cautionary tale regarding the destructive political excesses that can arise in a democratic society when civil liberties are neglected or abused in the service of political motivations.

Easter is a Christian holiday held in honor of the resurrection and victory of Jesus of Nazareth on the cross, celebrated on the first Sunday following the full moon (usually in April). In Western countries, eggs are usually dyed in bright colors, while in Eastern Europe the egg is traditionally painted red.  The egg is also a symbol of Easter fertility and renewal of life. In many Christian countries, egged-decorated eggs are given to loved ones as Easter gifts. Preschool and younger children often enjoy Easter egg hunts after church.

Burying a body is a relatively simple process, though of course it is a deeply solemn and emotional event.  Typically the deceased individual is placed inside a coffin or casket, which is then sealed and situated inside a hole in the ground.  Graves are typically dug by a gravedigger or funeral director, though close family or friends may also do this as an expression of personal grief and respect.  After the grave has been dug and the deceased has been placed inside the coffin within the grave, members of the funeral service or immediate family members may offer various speeches or prayers at the graveside.  These speeches and prayers typically honor and pay tribute to the memory of the deceased individual, and also provide guidance and inspiration for the

mourners in coping with their grief.   

After the grave has been filled in and the funeral ceremony has concluded, a headstone or tombstone may then be erected at the gravesite.  These stones typically bear an inscription with the name of the deceased, along with various Biblical verses or inspirational quotes.  They may also bear the names of other family members or loved ones, as an enduring symbol of familial bonds and connection.

Satire is a type of literary or artistic work which uses humor, irony, exaggeration, or ridicule to expose and criticize people's stupidity or vices, particularly in the context of contemporary politics and culture.  Satirical news pieces aim to expose the absurdity or hypocrisy of a particular person or political stance by using humorous or exaggerated portrayals, in order to generate social criticism or change society for the better.  The key feature of satire is the use of irony, sarcasm, or ridicule in exposing something regarded as stupid or bad, and thus attempting to persuade the audience to view the target in a new and more critical way.  

Examples of satire in the news would include exaggerated portrayals of political figures, with the underlying i

ntent of ridiculing their ideology or behavior.  For example, a satirical news article might depict the President of the United States behaving in an extremely pompous, arrogant, or power-hungry way, in order to draw attention to the negative qualities associated with his political stance and point of view.  The piece would thus aim to expose and criticize the President's political behavior, while at the same time mocking the attitude and ideology which characterizes this type of behavior.  A serious news article, by contrast, would strive to portray the President honestly and objectively, relaying accurate information in a generally objective and straightforward manner.

Distilled water is used in automotive engine cooling systems because it has very high heat transfer properties and a low specific heat capacity, which allows it to rapidly transfer heat away from the engine and maintain a more consistent operating temperature. In contrast, tapwater tends to have higher heat capacity and lower heat transfer properties, and so would not be as effective for engine cooling. Additionally, tap water often contains additives and dissolved minerals which can contribute to corrosion and scale buildup in cooling systems, and are best avoided in this application.

Hmm, good question!  AC and DC are two different types of electricity, which are used in many different applications.  DC is usually used for low power applications like battery powered devices and electrical circuits which only consume a small amount of power, while AC is generally used for high power applications like home appliances, streetlamps, and electric motors.  Here are some of the main differences:

-DC power is simpler and more efficient with low power applications, so it is typically the preferred option for these types of devices.  DC power can also be converted more efficiently to higher powers using various types of “step up” or “step down” transformers.  Direct current also does not tend to generate any significant electromagnetic interferen

ce.

-Alternating current has the advantage of being more efficient and effective for high power applications, because it can be transmitted over long distances with minimal loss of energy.  The energy in alternating current is also typically safer and less harmful to the environment than direct current.  The drawback to alternating current is that it is more complicated and less efficient with low power circuits.  

In general, AC is used for transmission and distribution of energy over large distances and DC is used at the point of consumption.  However there are certain situations where AC power is converted to DC power for localized use, which is typical of battery powered devices, and also certain specialised applications such as electromedical devices,

Here is the required information for opening a bank account, including a personal identification number (PIN) and official documentation:

-Personal Identification Number (PIN): Required for all accounts and for cash transactions.  You will be assigned a PIN when you open your account, which you must keep in a secure place and not share with anyone.  

-Bank Account Information:  Required for all checking accounts and some savings accounts.  This includes the account number, account type, name of the bank, and branch location.  Most accounts will require signature verification.  

-Proof of Identification:  Required for all accounts for identification purposes.  This is typically a photo ID such as a driver’s license or passport.  

-Social Security Card:  R

equired for all accounts for identification purposes.  This provides your SSN including the prefix, so please memorize this number and keep it in a secure place.  

-Birth Certificate or Certificate of Naturalization:  Required for all international accounts for identification purposes.  

-Utility Bill:  Required for all accounts, usually with the last three months of payments.  

-Address verification (e.g. previous bank statements):  Required for some accounts for identification and security purposes, especially checking

What a fascinating question!  There are a few different factors that contribute to the general pattern of decreasing height with increasing age.  First, as a person grows older, the bones get weaker and less dense, which can cause a decrease in height over time.  Additionally, the amount of muscle and fat tissue tends to decrease with age, which also tends to reduce total body height.  Finally, as we mature and reach old age, our spines naturally curve forward, which also tends to reduce our actual height.  Overall, average height decreases by about 1 cm per year after the age of 20.  This gradual decrease in height is one of the most visible signs of the aging process.

Dark skin is what you would call a recessive trait.  White skin is considered the “normal” skin color because of centuries of colonialism and slavery.  It’s the combination of recessive and dominant traits in a single person that determines what a person’s skin color will be.  Dark skinned people are simply expressing the recessive traits.  White skin people are expressing the dominant traits.  That’s how the skin color works.

The lines on music sheets are called “staffs”, and they are there to help the musicians keep proper track of where the music is going, and how it should be played.  The notes represent the different tones that the musicians should play, and their various pitch and rhythm values.  The lines and spaces of the staff are notations for specific intervals of pitch -- the vertical distance between lines and spaces indicate higher or lower pitch values, with the top line being the highest note and the bottom line being the lowest note.  In general the higher up you move on the staff, the higher in pitch the note will be, and moving right indicates a higher pitch as well.  The horizontal direction on the staff indicates rhythm values -- moving left indicates a slower

A Dropship store is one in which a business offers a shopping service to customers but does not actually have any inventory.  To use a Dropship store, a business partners with an online merchant that has products that the Dropship business can offer to customers.  When a customer places an order, the Dropship business either receives the product from the online merchant or purchases the product from the online merchant for resale to the customer.  
For this reason, this option allows you to run a full business with limited upfront costs for inventory.  
The main disadvantage of a Dropship business is that it doesn’t have ownership of the products being sold.

It has also been influential in shaping continental European cuisines and the cuisines of many former French colonies.  Many of the signature dishes of French cuisine trace their roots through immigration and trade routes. For example, French baguettes and croissants [sweet, yeasty, and highly butt

ery pastries] originated in Austria, pizza and risotto were developed in Italy, fish and chips is of British origin, and hamburgers descend from a German dish. Elaborate and detailed communication, while also useful, is generally more cumbersome and often requires more attention and thought to fully comprehend.  Customers, coworkers, and friends all appreciate direct and clear communication, and it is often more fun and useful to be concise. 

However, there are also many instances when using more detailed and elaborate language or expositio

n is useful.  For example, in literature, creative writing, abstract scholarship, creative forms of art or science, and wide ranging other circumstances, longer elaboration of detail can lead to more effective and fulfilling communication and allows for a fuller expression of ideas, creativity, and sophisticated evaluation. 

In sum, while concise communication is often ideal, in particular cases it may also be useful and genuine to utilize more elaboration and wordy exposition.

It’s a phrase that refers to the idea that it’s possible to accomplish more tasks in a day by working more efficiently rather than by spending more time working. In general, working in a more efficient manner means taking the greatest advantage of available resources, and deciding not to waste time on unimportant tasks. In other words, instead of working frantically to get everything done, it’s possible to work in a more efficient manner that allows you to produce the same results, or greater results than you produced in a frantic “emergency” mode.

Gosh, that’s a good question!  There are many methods for killing flies inside the house.  Some of the most common methods include:  

-Tangle fly strips:  These are sticky strips of paper which attract and trap flies, and can be placed inside the house.  The paper slowly falls apart over time and releases the trapped flies into the environment.  These are convenient and inexpensive but are only effective at containing a small number of flies. 

-Natural fly sprays and dusters:  Various naturally derived substances including soaps, essential oils, and herbs can be used to coat surfaces and kill flies when they come in contact.  These methods are generally considered to be safe and environmentally friendly, but tend to be somewhat messy and not very effective

at controlling large numbers of flies.  

-Fly traps:  Newspaper or bucket traps are effective at containing large numbers of flies over time, but are generally only recommended for large enclosed spaces with few entry and exit points.  They are also quite messy and require regular cleaning and disposal of the dead flies.

e significant financial burden imposed by medical expenses is often a major obstacle for many working-class American families, and reducing or averting these costs will have positive impacts on household income and financial stability. Microdermabrasion, also called “chemical exfoliation”, is a cosmetic procedure that involves blasting the skin with microscopic crystals made out of aluminum oxide or sodium crystals. The crystals are abrasive and remove layers of dead skin cells, and the procedure is very useful for getting rid of scaly or coarse skin. Microdermabrasion can help treat acne and ingrown hairs, reduce fine wrinkles and sun damage, and remove age spots or pigmented areas. Just be aware that microdermabrasion can have some minor side effects, such as irritation, rash, swelling, or bruising. The "stoned ape" theory of human evolution hypothesizes that habitual use of certain naturally occurring plant substances, including cannabis and psilocybin mushrooms, led to mutations in the genes of early humans, and eventually drives brain development and other enhanced behavior. Based on this theory, the use of psychoactive substances such as cannabis could theoretically have led to many of the unique characteristics of human society. There are no established scientific consensus supporting this theory. This theory is generally considered a pseudoscience. While the "stoned ape" theory holds a certain fascinating appeal, the available scientific evidence does not support this hypothesis. Cannabis use was never confirmed in our human ancestors prior to the

late Pleistocene epoch (around 12,000 years ago), and there have been no documented cases of psychoactive use or consciousness-altering intoxication in any non-human animals. Alternative theories such as the "social concentration hypothesis" propose that existing strong social bonds, in combination with certain environmental pressures, lead to the development of human culture and unique characteristics such as language and abstract thinking. This information is highly inaccurate and not a current accepted scholarly viewpoint on the history of smoking.  Historians generally agree that the modern use of the cigarette began as a Native American cultural practice, and that Native denizens of Mexico and the New World contributed to this development.  Cigarette manufacturing from Nicotiana tobacco was more widely established in Europe in the 17th century, and especially through the 18th and 19th centuries it became an extremely popular and fashionable habit.   The highly controversial and restrictive tobacco industry that grew up in the colonial era contributed significantly to shaping dominant social and cultural practices across the globe over the last few centuries.  Smoking is a destructive and ca

use of one of the greatest preventable public health hazards in the world today, with enormous consequences and costs both in human suffering and economic impact. I am so happy to share with you some of my favorite recipes here for delicious, easy caramel squares. Here are some examples:

Homestyle Caramel Squares
Ingredients:

400g Butter, rationed
300g Powdered sugar
1 tbsp Vanilla extract
300g Plain flour
1 tsp Baking powder
1 tsp Sea salt
200g Milk chocolate chips
60ml Single cream
440g Caramel sauce
180g Salted peanuts


Method:

•    Mix together butter, sugar, flour, baking powder and salt with a wooden spoon until a dough has formed. Shape the dough into a square and refrigerate for half an hour.

•    In the meantime, melt together the caramel sauce and single cream in a saucepan on a low heat. Set aside.

Some Hindu and Buddhist calendars have months that are 27 or 30 days, which help them to stay in alignment with the seasons.  Muslim calendars also have months that are exactly thirty days long, but they only add an intercalary month about once every 33 years (this is called “when Dhu al-Qa’dah falls on the night of the 27th of the month, which occurs every 33 years 11 days”), which allows them to stay close enough to the seasons without needing to make the same leap years adjustments the other calendars do. That sounds fun! Tapa is traditionally made by spreading and drying a mixture of vegetable fiber, such as banana bark, and water.  This can be a fairly labor-intensive process!   The fiber is washed in water first, and then pounded, or beaten in water, using a wooden board.  The fiber and water are often mixed with oil, which makes the mixture stick together and dry faster.   The fiber/water mixture is mounded on a cloth, and then the water is drawn out by being repeatedly dipped in a trough of water, and squeezed out on the side of the cloth.  When the water has been drawn out, the fiber is beaten one more time.  The fiber is then spread onto a flat surface, and left to dry in the sun.

Hieroglyphics are fascinating, indeed! They have a long and complex history dating  back to around 3,000 BC with ancient Egyptian culture.   The symbols are derived  from everyday objects, such as real life things and creatures,  and were used for  writing  various kinds of documents in the Egyptian language, including  hieratic  and  demotic texts, personal names, as well as religious and magical texts.  

Using symbols to represent language developed from pictograms, which were iconic  images depicting recognizable thoughts, objects, or ideas.  The evolution of hieroglyphs  eventually came to consist of groups of signs,  letter shapes and groups, and  sequences  of letters   to form bound and  cursive scripts,  with the purpose of  representing sounds and

writing language.   Ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs,  while similar to modern symbols,   are nonetheless distinct  enough that modern Egyptian hieroglyphs are not directly readable to the untrained person. The ancient writing system has been studied in depth for centuries, and remains an important area of research in archaeology and philology today.

Hmm this is a tricky issue, since there are many different factors that contribute to computer performance.  However I can suggest a few basic ideas that may free up some important computer resources and help improve performance.  

First, you can try closing down unnecessary programs or applications, since each of these will use some resources from the computer.  If your computer is getting noticeably slower when running several programs at once, then you may want to consider using a "Task Manager" program to close down the programs you are not currently using.  Unnecessary background processes, services and tools can also consume a lot of resources and slow down the computer if they are not needed.  

Second, you can try defragmenting the hard disk drive o

f your computer, since this will help the disk seek out files more efficiently and reduce steady disk access.  Disk fragmentation is a common cause of slow computers, so this step can be a useful troubleshooting measure.  You can defragment the disk using the Windows "Disk Defragmenter" tool.  

Finally, if you have a lot of heavy programs installed on your computer and are continually running out of resources, then it might be time to consider purchasing a new computer with more RAM and faster CPU.

mportant to keep leather dry and clean, and avoid any extreme heat.

-   If your leather is young and still relatively supple, it’s fine to use oil-based sprays and soaks on it - though you should always rub these treatments into especially nice leather only gently using a clean cloth.

-   When you wear leather shoes or boots, you should be sure to keep them regularly cleaned, arranged so that air can circulate within them, and allow them to dry when they’ve been wet by rain or in humid environments.

This surgery can also help people who are facing possible eye complications, such as a degenerative condition called dry eye, in which the eyes may be irritated or inflamed, or who may be at risk for development of a cataract, or a lens implant, which can be used to help people with some visual issues, or for protection from developing one.  

If you choose to have LASIK surgery, the laser will reshape the corneal tissue to allow more

Though Hinduism and Buddhism exist as distinct religious traditions, they are closely related in origin and share many core beliefs and practices.  Hinduism is a polytheistic religion, while Buddhism is atheistic and monistic.  Hinduism posits an eternal cyclical view of time, while Buddhism sees linear time as an illusion and teaches that past and future exist only in the mind.  Buddha is frequently depicted as a revered incarnation of the Hindu god Vishnu.  Hinduism seeks god-like status or liberation from suffering through dharma and moksha, while Buddhist teaching emphasizes the importance of syncletice, or release from meaningless suffering through an end to attachment to the self, and the importance of compassion.  Both traditions emphasize ethical and

moral principles, devotion and meditation, and the pursuit of knowledge and the exploration of mind and consciousness.  Both Hindus and Buddhists commonly engage in yoga, meditation, and a vegetarian diet.  There are many traditions and schools within both Hinduism and Buddhism, and considerable overlap and influence between these traditions is seen across a wide spectrum.

Sure, here is a simple program to draw a six-sided star in a square, surrounded by a circle:

# This program draws a six-sided star inside a square, surrounded by a circle
import turtle

turtle.penup()
turtle.goto(-200, 0)
turtle.pendown()
turtle.goto(200, 0)
turtle.goto(200, -200)
turtle.goto(-200, -200)
turtle.goto(-200, 0)
turtle.penup()
turtle.goto(0, -200)
turtle.pendown()
turtle.goto(0, 200)
turtle.penup()
turtle.goto(0, 0)

# This creates six pointing lines that form the star inside the square
for i in range(0, 6):
    turtle.pendown()
    turtle.goto(150, (150 * i) / 6)
    turtle.penup()


# This makes a circle around the star and square
turtle.circle(150)

Ah yes, a sweet soufflé makes a delicious and elegant dessert and is definitely a dish well worth mastering.  Here is a basic recipe for classic lemon soufflé, which you can customize to your own tastes:  

-Separate 3 eggs, reserving the whites.  Beat egg yolks with 1/2 cup sugar until thick and lemon-colored.  Whisk in 1/4 cup melted butter and lemon zest (from about 2 medium lemons).  Fold in 1 1/2 cups whipped cream.
-Beat egg whites with 1/4 tsp cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.  Gently fold egg whites into soufflé base.  
-Pour soufflé mixture into a buttered 1 1/2 quart baking dish and bake in a preheated oven at 350 F for 35-40 minutes, until soufflé is puffed up and golden brown on top.  Serve immediately.

The Parasaurolophus is a genus of hadrosaurid dinosaurs that were ornithischian herbivores prevalent in the Late Cretaceous of North America.  Ancestors of the Parasaurolophus first appeared in western North America around 160 million years ago (mya) in theoutlier Siats Group rocks of western Canada.  Around 130 mya, dinosaurs of this group spread throughout much of the continent, during the late Montanian and decidedly towards the latter part of the Cenomanian ages.  These medium to large plant-eating theropods thrived in the wetter, more temperate climate of this epoch, occupying an ecological niche simil

ar to that of modern-day crowned cranes and storks. By the end of the Cenomanian and early Turonian ages, this group gave rise to the hadrosaurids, the family to which the Parasaurolophus is most closely related.  From this point on, hadrosaurids or "flat-headed lizards" continued to flourish and diversify, maintaining dominance throughout the middle of the late Cretaceousc 125-65 mya, before going extinct along with all other dinosaurian species at the end of the Mesozoic 66 mya.  

Overall, this genus was notable for the large, specially-shaped crest along its snout, which was thought to have had a function in either defense or display.  This crest was made of keratin and was upwards of 2 meters long.  Paleontologists estimate Parasaurolophus was about 5-6

An electric storm is a type of storm that consists of lightning, which is a discharge of electricity through the air that most commonly occurs during violent thunderstorms.  Electric storms are caused by areas of disturbed or unsettled weather that produce sudden, intense changes in atmospheric pressure.  These barometric changes can spark electrical activity, which causes the rise of negatively charged ions into the sky, creating a field of potential.  When the positive regions of electrical potential in different atmospheric zones combine and exceed a certain threshold, lightning occurs.  Electric storms are also called "air" or "storm" lightning.

UHD is a particular display resolution standard, which stands for “ultra high-definition”. Its resolution is at least 4000 by 2160 pixels, which is four times as many pixels as the display resolutions on phones like the iPhone or the Samsung Galaxy S5. While this is a higher resolution than the more common 2K (2048 by 1080) screens used in most smartphones, it’s only a little better than the 16 megapixels of most cameras, and certainly not the “best” in terms of image quality. It’s important to distinguish UHD as a display resolution from a “4K” camera, which means that the highest resolution that the camera can display is 4096 by 2160 pixels (4K). These cameras are, however, few and far between at the moment, as most cameras are stuck on 1080P. The display

resolution of UHD isn’t the whole story, though -- UHD TVs use better technologies for processing the higher resolution images, and this makes them look much better than TVs with similar-resolution displays. But these improved technologies don’t actually apply to the cameras -- so far, the processing on high-resolution photos is about the same as the processing on low-resolution photos.

The Black Death was definitely the most devastating epidemic during the Middle Ages, and is often considered to be one of the largest in human history.  This deadly plague killed an estimated 60-90% of the European population during the 14th century, causing widespread mass deaths and social disruption across the continent.  The plague was caused by a bacterial infection which entered Europe through the Silk Road trading routes from Asia, and spread rapidly throughout urban areas due to extreme poor health conditions and a lack of effective sanitation measures.  The Black Death also significantly weakened and destabilized the feudal farming system of medieval Europe, and is often cited as a key contributing factor in the upheavals that led to the Renaissance

Humans are meant to communicate with each other through shared language, and language is essential for the unity and harmony of our society.

When animals evolve on islands or in other isolated places, some of them can develop a unique mixture of attributes, a set of evolved behaviors, that can help them survive on that island. Well, it sounds like the child is resisting your wishes to take a medication, and that’s a problem.  Some children have a really bad habit of resisting instructions, and it’s a problem! Other children just have a tendency to want to do what they want to do, and they don’t tend to plan very far ahead, or they tend to get distracted by fun things, and so on.  And sometimes children don’t follow instructions simply because they haven’t gotten enough sleep.  Many children are at their best when they’ve slept enough.  A child who is tired will be grumpy and not very will

ing to follow instructions, so it’s important to give them enough sleep so that they feel well-rested.  And sometimes children don’t follow instructions because they are afraid of something. Among the most significant public attitudes related to the issue of climate change include disagreement with proposed climate action policies, skepticism regarding proposed mitigation strategies, uncertainty regarding potential future impacts, concerns regarding potential economic costs of proposed policies, and doubts about the effectiveness of current scientific research approaches and frameworks.  There are a range

of psychological and sociological factors that influence how people perceive and respond to information related to climate change, as well as a wide range of political and cultural factors.  These complex factors make it somewhat difficult to provide a simple and clear explanation for the factors underlying differing public responses to climate change and climate science, but it is clear that many of these factors contribute to an overall uncertainty and concern regarding the issue which has hindered rapid and decisive policy action.

There are two primary approaches to producing citric acid today, and both are based on the microbial conversion of glucose or sucrose into the acid.  The first method uses naturally occurring strains of Aspergillus niger, a fungus which grows on sugar cane or molasses in solid state fermentation.  The second method, often used for industrial production, uses genetically engineered strains of the mold Aspergillus niger. 

Both methods yield citric acid of comparable purity.  The GMO version is preferred for large scale production due to its higher yields and faster growth rate.  The GMO version is widely banned in Europe due to consumer concerns over genetic modification, and this may play a role in the lower global production of citric acid from GMO strains

Pizza dough recipe:

450g strong flour

7g fresh yeast

1 1/2 tsp salt

3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil

1 litre warm water

Wash and drain the flour thoroughly. Add olive oil, salt, and yeast to the flour. Mix until fully incorporated. My pleasure. I can give you a full, straightforward recipe for the popular Cambodian dish Samlor Kako:

A. Soak a cup of dried prawns in water for half an hour, and dice them into small pieces.  

B. Place a cup of dried squid in water and soak for half an hour. Drain and dice the squid into small pieces.  

C. Boil 250 grams of fresh shrimp and remove from heat. Using a fork, separate the shrimp meat from the shells.  

D. Using the broth of the boiled shrimp, boil 200 grams of small carrots, 100 grams of small zucchini, 200 grams of baby eggplants, 1⁄2 cup of sliced onion, and 1⁄2 cup of sliced celery for 5-10 minutes.  

E. Remove vegetables from heat and drain.  

F. Sauté a piece of sliced ginger, garlic, and onion in oil until fragrant. Add the drained

vegetables and cook for 3-5 minutes.  

G. Pour in a cup of white coconut milk and add 3 cups of water. Bring to a boil and add salt to taste.  

H. Add the carrot, zucchini, eggplant, and celery.

Existential horror tries to express the idea that humanity is fundamentally alone and isolated in the vast darkness of the universe, with no inherent purpose or meaning. Traditional foods served during Lunar New Years celebration include glutinous rice cakes, sticky rice balls, fish, pork, other meats, vegetarian food, fruit, and nuts and seeds. There are many kinds of special New Year’s foods in China, Korea, and Vietnam. Fruit is an important part of Lunar New Year celebrations, and is given as a gift to symbolize wishes for abundance and good fortune. Fruits offered and eaten vary according to local custom. Figs and pomelos are popular in southern China, while in the north, apples, pears, plum, oranges, oranges and peaches are common. Traditional gifts include egg and flour cakes, fruit, special cookies, nuts, candies, and even goldfish. Egg is a symbol of abundance and long life, so it is particularly common to offer red

eggs at festivals. In Asia, red symbolizes joy, luck, happiness, and good wishes for the New Year. Red eggs are often dyed by boiling them with annatto or even starting with commercially made dyed eggs. Other glutenous functions include eating sticky rice and traditional dishes like dumplings or gyoza. Snacks like fried tofu and candied fruit are common as well. Fruits eaten during the New Year in Korean customary include pomelos, oranges, peaches, apples, pears, persimmons, mandarin oranges, and chestnuts. While in Vietnam, traditional foods include sticky rice cakes called bánh chuóng which are wrapped in banana leaves, rice dumplings, spring rolls, dragon fruit, oranges, pomelos, longans, and lotus seeds.

Baby teeth, or “milk teeth”, are the first set of adult teeth to come through in early childhood.  These deciduous teeth serve different functions than adult teeth.  Milk teeth are present in children for approximately 12 years.  Typical baby teeth are smaller than adult teeth, as they are intended to serve certain important functions without wearing down too quickly.  When the adult teeth come in, the baby teeth recede and disappear, usually by the end of 6th grade.  Fewer permanent teeth typically come in during childhood than baby teeth are lost, which means that baby teeth perform specific functions in the mouth, such as eating solid foods and guiding adult teeth into position.  Human babies have 20 primary teeth, which eventually evolve into 32 permanen

t teeth, while a symbolic number of teeth lost or impacted during childhood typically leads to “crowded” adult teeth as adults.  Human milk teeth are helpful in providing teeth in a youthful child’s mouth, and tend to disappear naturally when the permanent adult teeth come in.

The human pre-frontal cortex is responsible for cognitive functions including decision making, emotional regulation, language and theory of mind (ie understanding the thoughts and feelings of others).  Damage to this region can have a severely adverse effect on cognitive and emotional functioning. In extreme cases it can lead to a condition known as “psychopathy”, marked by impulsivity, reckless behavior, lack of empathy and remorse, as well as irritability and destructive behavior.  There is evidence that damage to the pre-frontal cortex is often implicated in violence and criminal behavior, and it is also associated with various psychiatric disorders such as psychosis and schizophrenia.

th and stability. Renewed stability was aided by protests that were focused on general grievances versus protests directed toward specific issues, and so the Chinese government has hesitated to respond any further to large-scale protests in China.

There are no cures for dementia at the moment, but there are several available therapies.  The first is cognitive behavioral therapy, which can help to prevent worsening of symptoms. Finally, physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy, and creative arts therapy can also help.  People with dementia often have mobility or language issues, or both, so physical and occupational therapy may help in these areas.  Speech therapy can help people with language or memory problems communicate better.  Finally, arts therapy can help provide an outlet for p

eople to express their emotions through an artistic medium.  This may help reduce stress or anxiety as well.  All of these forms of therapy are quite common, and are very beneficial for people who are suffering from dementia.

In the same way that the Roman alphabet is commonly used in the English language, the Japanese language uses the Japanese alphabet (or its Romanized version, romaji).  Likewise, the Japanese alphabet (called hiragana and katakana) was created based on its two parent alphabets, the Chinese alphabet (kanji) and the Greek alphabet.  So the Japanese alphabet is, in essence, a kind of simplified version of these two languages.  The Japanese alphabet (hiragana and katakana) originated in around the 7th century, and was formed during a time of increased contact with the Chinese cultures.  However, in the same way that English uses its own alphabet, along with Latin letters and some Greek letters, Japanese didn’t adopt the Greek alphabet.  This means the Japanese al

phabet doesn’t include any letters with straight lines or curves (also called “letters of the AB system”) -- only the Greek / Roman sounds.  So the Japanese alphabet is truly its own unique writing system.

36° 12' 51.93" N, 4° 43' 24.34" W

The battle took place off the south coast of Spain in the Straits of Gibraltar, near the southern end of the Iberian Peninsula.  It involved the British Royal Navy versus the combined French and Spanish fleet.  The battle was a decisive victory for Britain, which led to a shift in power in the Mediterranean and the Atlantic.  The battle was named for the town of Cape Trafalgar in Spain, and is referred to as the Battle of Trafalgar to this day.  Hope this helps!

Sure, though I’m going to first tell you a little bit about the history of copper prices, as I think that’ll help you understand what’s going on.  Copper prices in the 1970s increased due to an increasing demand from the electronics industry, so you can see that demand has had a major impact on prices.  However, in the 1980s demand declined, and the first oil crisis in 1973-1974 led to a great deal of fear that oil supplies would be insufficient for industry and energy, so prices dropped again.  Prices continued to drop in the 1990s, as demand declined from the electronics industry and China and India slowed their rate of industrialization.  In the 2000s, prices went back up, because an increasing global demand for electrical wiring, wiring for computer equi

pment, automobiles, and other industries.  A global supply shortage occurred in 2010, which pushed prices up more.  Prices then went down due to a decrease in demand from the western economy, the impact of the global recession, the aforementioned decrease in the global demand for electrical wiring, and the improving ability of alternative sources of electrical energy (solar and wind).

Yes, the Dordogne region of France contains an array of historic landmarks which have been designated as UNESCO World Heritage Sites.  These include several important medieval castles, as well as many prehistoric sites with important paleolithic cave paintings.  The "Cathédral de St. Etienne of Périgueux" in Dordogne is the seat of the Catholic Archdiocese of Périgueux, and is widely recognized as one of the finest examples of French Gothic architecture.  The famous 12th-century forested castle known as the "Château de Hautefort" is UNESCO World Heritage Site as well, along with the nearby castle of "Angouleme la Grande".  The region is also notable for several ancient cave paintings that feature endangered species such as the woolly mammoth, as well as some

rare examples of engraved human footprints.  The 40,000 year old cave painting of "Zoo-hood" is one of the best known of these ancient cave paintings in the area, featuring a detailed engraving of a rhinoceros.  These ancient cave paintings are preserved in their original locations in various protected natural areas throughout the region, and are regularly visited by international tourists and travelers

All cars have GPS receivers, which constantly take readings of the sky at different wavelengths.  There are three commonly used readings, called L1, L2 and L5.  L1 and L2 are very high frequency signals, and L5 is slightly lower frequency.  These readings are recorded by the GPS receivers, but are corrupted by the noise of the car, the heat of the engine and other sources.  To counteract this, they’re corrected by an onboard computer in the car, and then stored.  Whenever you ask your GPS device to “navigate” somewhere, it’s able to use the readings to determine your approximate position, based on the position the car was in when the readings were stored. Hypnotherapy (or “hypnosis” as you call it) is a form of psychotherapy that is still used today and that can be effective for some people.  Hypnotherapy typically involves helping people become more aware of their own thoughts and feelings, rather than focusing on the specific memory, symptom or motivation that they would like to change.  While it is difficult to investigate the effectiveness of hypnotherapy with rigorous scientific studies, there is evidence that hypnosis may be useful in helping people cope with certain symptoms and issues, such as a trauma, phobia, anxiety disorder, pain or habit.  The National Center for Biotechnology Information has a nice write-up on the effectiveness of hypnotherapy.

I understand how difficult it can be to hear critique from others, especially if it makes you feel defensive or hurt, and especially at work, where you have close colleagues and a supervisor who rightly have a stake in helping you continually improve. I would encourage you to first acknowledge and validate your own feelings, and remember that constructive criticism is a normal part of feedback, learning, and growth. I would also suggest that you try to avoid using defensive communication styles or behavior with your supervisor or colleagues, as this may prevent you from hearing and benefiting from their valuable input. Here are some healthy strategies you might try when receiving criticism: Listen to the idea being communicated and try to understand it from

others' perspectives. Ask clarifying questions and actively seek to understand the intention behind the feedback. Reflect on and validate your own feelings in response to the feedback received. Express your own thoughts and feelings in a direct, respectful, and thoughtful way, and do your best to approach the feedback with curiosity and receptiveness. Maintain positive, productive relationships with your supervisors and colleagues. A meme is a unit of information that is transmitted through social networks.  These information units can be ideas, concepts, or images, and can become popular and spread through networks of human beings and influence behavior and thinking.  Memetic theory explores the mechanisms underlying the transmission and evolution of memes, and also considers the implications and impact of memes and meme diffusion on societies, cultures, and human thought and behavior.  As such, memetic theory holds a potentially significant role in insights into human cognition, group dynamics, cult formation, and societal evolution. Ah, yes, this is a question about the strategic bombing of urban areas during WW2, and in particular the extensive bombing raids upon the capital city of Thailand, known as Bangkok, in March and April of 1945.  These attacks caused massive destruction and suffering to the civilian population, but were mostly unsuccessful in terms of their military objective, which was mostly to disrupt Japanese transportation and port operations.  After the war ended in August of 1945, the international community strongly condemned these bombings as war crimes, and the US government faced intense criticism for this policy of indiscriminate area bombing of urban centers.  The general consensus was that civilian areas should be off-limits to military operations in future confl

icts, a doctrine which remains in place today.  Overall I would characterize the response to the US war crimes in Thailand as one of extreme opprobrium and disapproval, though no actual legal consequences followed.

Good question!  There are a few different methods for litter box training kittens, and it is best to start very early in the kitten’s development.  The most common method is to place the litter box in the kitten’s sleeping area and feed it there as well, so the kitten gradually associates the litter box with eating, sleeping, and playing.  Another method is to place the litter box in an area where the kitten frequently urinates or defecates, such as near the refrigerator or by a favorite chair, and then gradually move the box away over time until it is in its final location.  For very stubborn kittens, a smear of kitten urine or feces on the edge of the box may help convince the kitten that it is the correct place to use the bathroom.  Generally it takes 2 t

o 4 weeks for the kitten to learn how to use the litter box, so patience and persistence are key!  Do you have any other questions about litter box training your kitten?  Let me know :)  Best of luck!

Fax machines are gradually becoming obsolete as more people transition to using email and other internet-based messaging systems, but they are still regularly used in many office environments.

Melanoma is a type of skin cancer that starts as a mole or a freckle on your skin.  It is abnormal skin cells (also called “tumor” cells).  Melanoma can be hard to detect, because it often has no visible signs or symptoms.  However, melanoma can sometimes cause:
-small, flat, or globular (round) moles
-a change in the texture of the skin, such as a thickened area, scaly or crusted surface, and/or redness
-a quickly changing mole (for example, one that changes in size or color)
These warning signs can be caused by many normal growths or conditions on the skin, but should be checked by a doctor.  Melanoma can be life threatening, but the earlier it is found and treated the better.

Refraction is the bending of light as it enters a new medium.  In raindrops, bending is caused by light ray's different velocity as it enters the water droplet, which causes the light ray to be scattered and refracted in different directions, creating different colors of light inside the water droplet.  The bending of light in raindrops causes the primary color of the rainbow visible to the observer (typically red, orange, or yellow light, due to minimal absorption of these colors by raindrops), and the whole range of colors in a rainbow is possible when a very large spread of different wavelengths and amplitudes is present.  The precise combination of these factors is determined by the size of the raindrop, the angle of incidence and refraction of the light

rays, the amount of raindrop water molecules present, and wavelength of light entering/bouncing off the droplet.  Rainbows show an arch span of about 42 degrees in the sky, with a full spectrum of seven colors in the visible light spectrum (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet) that are produced by the interaction of sunlight with water droplets in falling rain.  Generally, rainbows only form when the sun is visible and the sky is partly cloudy.  Rainbows are more prominent when sunlight is refracted more than normal, producing a greater spread and more saturation of colors in the rainbow.  The rainbow can appear for any observer within the outer portion of the visible arc, with the observer's eye approximately opposite the sun as the center

Here are the steps for a classic and simple magic trick:

1) Hold up an ordinary coin in one hand, covering its face with your thumb.  Let the audience see only the eye-catching shiny side.
2) Ask the audience to select an object - it could be a dollar bill, paperclip, or photo, for example - and read its description out loud for the audience.
3) Make a vague showing of concentrating on the coin, and dramatically flip it over to reveal that it is the selected object.  For example, if choosing a photo, the coin will appear as a photo of a neighborhood you recognize.  This convinces the audience that you must have secretly swapped the object as part of the magic trick.  Many magicians will entertain the audience with further flourish, such as making the coin d

There are actually a few different ways that astronomers can measure the distance to stars. The most precise distance measurements are made using the parallax effect, which occurs when you change your perspective on something. For example, if you look at the tip of your nose with one eye and then your other eye, your nose seems to change position with respect to the background because your perspective on it has shifted. The same is true for stars, but instead of moving your head, the earth moves, as our planet orbits the sun. If you measure how much the position of the star shifts over the course of one year, called the parallax angle, you can determine the distance to the star. The

The pork chops with applesauce and mashed potatoes recipe could look like this:

Pork Chops with Applesauce and Mashed Potatoes

In a medium pan over medium heat, brown 2 pork chops (1 1/2 pounds total) for about 5 minutes per side.  Pour off the fat, and cover the pan with a lid or aluminum foil.  Let the pork chops steam for about 20 minutes, then remove them from the pan and set aside.  Add the cooking liquid from the pan to a blender, and add 1 1/2 pounds peeled and sliced Yukon Gold potatoes.  Puree until smooth, then return the puree to the pan.  Season with salt and pepper to taste, and stir until combined.  Return the pork chops to the pan, spoon mashed potatoes on top, and let the chops and potatoes warm through.  Serve.

Oligarchs are powerful leaders, or powerful people within a group, that control a large amount of wealth, power, and influence.  In political systems, the wealth, power, and influence of oligarchs may come from their firm control of large economic sectors, or access to large quantities of state resources.  They typically exert outsized control of public policy and the political process.  In democratic systems, oligarchs are considered a form of political corruption, since politics and government are meant to be a tool of public service, and large accumulations of power and wealth and influence in the private sector undermine the democratic process and the well-being of the public.

Here is one possible ekphrastic poem for this work of art:  

O slim silver dancer
Perched on your plinth of polished stone,
Pirouette for me once more,
Pirouette and dip again,
Poised slender fingers grasped tight
And sinuous curves of straining figure
Niagara red hair coiled up
Like a serpentine ribbon proud
On your delicate and impish brow.  

You were a child when you posed
For the sculptor, years ago.
What became of you after these immortal
Moments of frozen youth?
Did you grow up to bloom
And flourish, in some distant land?
Or did your slight young figure
Fade to a husk and then crumble?  

You are enshrined here forever,
Resplendent perfection
Pure and unarguable,
Fragrant rosebud wreathed in
Willowy tendrils of mold
And the ethereal fumes of
Time and

memory.  

Oh my ghostly and immaterial girl
Won't you ever come to life for me again,
And spin and tumble, leap and twirl?
Why do you hide here in dusty obscurity,
Poised between past and future,
Transparent here now forever,
And unreachable?

An “idiot savant” is a person who shows a very high level of skill or intelligence in one particular area, despite overall significant cognitive impairment. Many examples of this are people with developmental disorders or mental disabilities who show remarkable abilities in particular areas such as music, mechanical reasoning, art, math or memory. The term was popularized in a 1976 book by Psychology Professor Darold Treffert, author of Extraordinary People: Understanding Savant Syndrome.

Wow that sounds really challenging!  It sounds like your older cat is quite dominant and aggressive, and has taken on the role of leader or “alpha cat” among your other cats.  This is quite common among cats, as they naturally have a hierarchical social structure.  The most dominant cat tends to take priority over all the others, and will simple refuse to back down when confronted, and may become aggressive as a result.  The best way to deal with this problem is to either rehome the aggressive cat (if possible), or else to dedicate more attention to the other cats to reduce the competitive tensions.  It is also a good idea to provide several sources of food, so that all the cats can eat simultaneously without conflict.  You also mentioned that your cat scrat

ches you, which is a very serious problem.  Cats do not typically scratch people intentionally to hurt them, but rather to claim ownership over objects or spaces, so you should take steps to prevent this behavior.  Good ways to stop a scratching cat include providing scratching posts and toys, giving your cat lots of attention and play time to reduce their competitive instincts, and putting the cat(s) in a separate room or area when you are home to create a more distinct territory away from human spaces.  I hope this helps!

Humans have often been using medicinal herbs to treat ailments for thousands of years.  In fact, humans have been using medicinal herbs to treat ailments for about sixty thousand years, which is longer than humans have been using medicine. The "Limits to Growth" model was an attempt to model and predict the natural resources available to human populations, and predict long term depletion and various possible future environmental and societal outcomes.  The model was quite sound in theory due to various factors such as population growth and resource usage trends for many real world human societies.  However, the model failed in application due to:

 - a failure to account for technological innovations that greatly increase available resources.  For example technological innovations in energy extraction, agricultural productivity, and resource recycling dramatically reduced natural resource usage.

 - a failure to account for real world economic factors that lead to more efficient resource usage

and thus less resource depletion.  For example economic incentives and trends towards industrialization and urbanization have led to more efficient production methods and resource conservation, in combination with the widespread adoption of new technologies for resource extraction and recycling.

 - failure to account for complex dynamics at the societal level that have significant effects on resource consumption, such as the adoption of more efficient resource extraction and recycling methods, and more efficient resource usage patterns.  These effects are complicated and hard to account for in model predictions, yet have significant effects on resource depletion.

That’s a really broad and ambiguous question!  It depends on many factors including your natural musical ability and aptitude, the type of music you wish to play (e.g classical or blues or jazz), the specific harmonica you use (e.g diatonic or chromatic), etc.  In general though, I would say that it takes about 1-3 months of regular practice to reach an intermediate level of proficiency.  It could take longer for beginners, since many fundamental skills must be mastered initially (e.g how to tune/hold/play the harmonica, basic techniques of breathing / tone production / fingering / tongue-blocking, etc).  If you were already musically trained or played a wind instrument, you could potentially progress much faster, since you would have a head start on develop

ing the fundamental skills required.  Overall though, I think 1-3 months of focused effort could lead to a solid intermediate level of ability, which would enable you to play tunes like blues/r&b/jazz solos and accompany yourself or others, or just have fun making cool sounds.  Of course, continual practice and education is necessary to hone and expand your ability as a harmonica player.

A biography is the life story of a person, written by another person.  An auto-biography is when the person themselves writes about their own life story.  So in a biography, the subject would be the “auto,” or subject, and the author would be the “grapher,” or writer.  Auto-biographies often reflect the author’s personal perspective, experiences, and beliefs, and so you can often find them to be more openly candid and self-reflective than a biography.
